symptoms: Pain radiating to arm, jaw, back
signs: Signs of heart failure (elevated JVP, pulmonary crackles)

components.name: Recent severe angina (≥ 2 events in 24 hrs)
≥ 1mm concordant ST elevation in any lead
≥ 5mm discordant ST elevation in any lead

management_steps.action: ABCs, general appearance, focused physical examination
12 & 15 lead ECG within 10 minutes of arrival
Aspirin 300mg chewed stat (unless contraindicated - give Clopidogrel 300mg stat po if aspirin hypersensitivity)
Nitroglycerin (check ECG first for RV infarct): Nitrolingual spray 1 spray q5min (max 3 in 15min) OR sublingual 1 tablet q5min (max 3 doses)
Fentanyl IV 50mcg aliquots if discomfort not relieved by nitroglycerin
O2 via nasal prongs @ 2L/min if O2 sats < 94%, titrate
IV access, bloods (Troponin I, U&E, FBC, VBG)
If onset < 12 hours AND continuous chest pain → thrombolysis (Actilyse or Tenecteplase)

Heparin (Clexane 30mg IV stat then 1mg/kg SC after 15min OR unfractionated heparin 60IU/kg IV stat then infusion), Clopidogrel 300mg stat po, Simvastatin 40mg stat po, Beta-blocker if no contraindications
High risk: same adjunctive therapy as STEMI + admit HCU/Medicine + cardiology consult
Serial troponin and ECG at 3 hours (hs) or 6 hours (stat lab). If delta troponin negative, discharge with OPD follow-up within 1 week

drugs.notes: Chewed. Contraindicated in hypersensitivity - use Clopidogrel instead
If aspirin hypersensitivity or as adjunct in STEMI/NSTE-ACS
Contraindicated if SBP < 90, HR < 50 or > 100, RV infarction, PDE inhibitor use
For pain not relieved by nitroglycerin
LMWH alternative: unfractionated heparin 60IU/kg IV stat (max 4000IU) then 12IU/kg/hr infusion (max 1000IU/hr)
Contraindicated in haemodynamic instability, heart failure, pulmonary oedema, SBP < 100, HR ≤ 60, AV block 2°/3°, asthma

warnings: Time of onset of symptoms or maximum pain must be < 12 hours for thrombolysis
Consider aortic dissection in central chest pain radiating to back with tearing sensation, differential radial pulse, differential arm BP > 15mmHg, widened mediastinum on CXR
RV infarction (associated with inferior MI): ST elevation in V4R - avoid nitrates
True posterior MI: tall R in V1 and STE ≥ 0.5mm in V7-9
protocols.disposition: STEMI: Refer to Internal Medicine/Cardiology urgently. NSTE-ACS high risk: Admit HCU/Medicine. Low risk: May discharge with OPD follow-up within 1 week if serial troponins negative.
protocols.notes_updates:
Symptoms: Chest pain or pressure, Dyspnoea, Diaphoresis, Nausea/vomiting, Pain radiating to arm, jaw, back, Syncope or pre-syncope
Signs: Tachycardia, Hypotension, Diaphoresis, New cardiac murmur, Signs of heart failure (elevated JVP, pulmonary crackles), Cardiogenic shock
Risk factors: Age ≥ 65 years, Smoking, Family history of CAD, Diabetes Mellitus, Hypercholesterolaemia, Hypertension, Previous MI, Drug abuse: cocaine, methamphetamine, HIV ± HAART (protease inhibitors)
Investigations - bedside: 12 & 15 lead ECG (including V4R, V7-9), Vital signs and O2 sats, Finger prick glucose, VBG
Investigations - lab: Troponin I (hs or stat lab), U&E, FBC
Investigations - imaging: Portable CXR, CTPA if PE suspected
Disposition: STEMI: Refer to Internal Medicine/Cardiology urgently. NSTE-ACS high risk: Admit HCU/Medicine. Low risk: May discharge with OPD follow-up within 1 week if serial troponins negative.
Warnings: Door-to-needle time for thrombolysis: 30 minutes; Time of onset of symptoms or maximum pain must be < 12 hours for thrombolysis; Consider aortic dissection in central chest pain radiating to back with tearing sensation, differential radial pulse, differential arm BP > 15mmHg, widened mediastinum on CXR; RV infarction (associated with inferior MI): ST elevation in V4R - avoid nitrates; True posterior MI: tall R in V1 and STE ≥ 0.5mm in V7-9

absolute: Known structural cerebral vascular lesion (e.g., AVM)
Ischaemic stroke within 3 months (except acute ischaemic stroke within 3 hours)
Significant closed head or facial trauma within 3 months
relative: History of chronic, severe, poorly controlled hypertension
SBP > 180mmHg or DBP > 110mmHg (lower BP with Nitrocine infusion first)
Traumatic or prolonged CPR (> 10min) or major surgery (< 3 weeks)
Active peptic ulcer disease (bled within past 2-4 weeks)
Prior Streptokinase exposure or allergic reaction

signs: ST elevation on ECG consistent with STEMI

management_steps.action: Confirm STEMI diagnosis and thrombolysis eligibility
management_steps.details: Onset < 12 hours, continuous chest pain, no contraindications
management_steps.caution: Check all absolute and relative contraindications

management_steps.action: Heparin (Clexane 30mg IV stat then 1mg/kg SC after 15min)
management_steps.details: OR unfractionated heparin 60IU/kg IV stat then infusion

management_steps.caution: Avoid in haemodynamic instability, heart failure, SBP < 100, HR ≤ 60, AV block, asthma

drugs.notes: Follow manufacturer dosing guidelines for STEMI
Contraindicated in haemodynamic instability, heart failure, pulmonary oedema, SBP < 100, HR ≤ 60, AV block 2°/3°, asthma

protocols.disposition: Admit to CCU/HCU for monitoring post-thrombolysis. Cardiology follow-up.
protocols.notes_updates: Follow manufacturer weight-based dosing for Alteplase. Monitor closely for bleeding and reperfusion arrhythmias.

management_steps.action: IV access, bloods (U&E, FBC, Troponin, VBG)

management_steps.details: Double dose if on chronic diuretics
Start at 0.5 mcg/kg/min, titrate to effect

management_steps.action: CPAP/BiPAP if persistent hypoxia or tiring

drugs.notes: Double dose if on chronic diuretics
Titrate to effect. Avoid if SBP < 90

warnings: Monitor for hypotension with GTN and diuretics
CO2 retention in COPD patients on high-flow O2
protocols.disposition: Admit to HCU/CCU. Cardiology review.
protocols.notes_updates: Consider CPAP/BiPAP early if failing standard therapy. Look for underlying cause (MI, arrhythmia, hypertensive emergency).

management_steps.action: IV access x2, bloods including lactate and cultures
management_steps.details: Blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics if possible

management_steps.action: Vasopressors if MAP < 65 after fluid resuscitation

management_steps.details: Drain abscesses, remove infected lines, surgical debridement

warnings: 1-hour bundle: lactate, blood cultures, antibiotics, fluids
Do not delay antibiotics for cultures if patient unstable
protocols.disposition: Admit to ICU/HCU.
protocols.notes_updates: Sepsis 6 bundle: O2, IV access, fluids, cultures, lactate, antibiotics within 1 hour.

risk_factors: Known allergy (foods, drugs, insect stings, latex)

management_steps.details: Repeat every 5 min if no improvement

drugs.notes: First-line. Lateral thigh. Repeat every 5 min if needed

warnings: Adrenaline IM is first-line - never delay
IV adrenaline only in cardiac arrest setting
Biphasic reactions can occur 4-8 hours later
protocols.disposition: Observe 6-12 hours minimum. Discharge with EpiPen if allergic cause identified.
protocols.notes_updates: Adrenaline IM 0.5mg (1:1000) into lateral thigh, repeat q5min. Observe 6-12 hours for biphasic reaction.

management_steps.details: Repeat once after 10 min if still seizing

management_steps.action: Refractory: Midazolam infusion or thiopental + intubation

warnings: Do not delay treatment - permanent brain damage after 30 min
Prepare for intubation with phenobarbital/refractory cases
protocols.disposition: Admit to ICU/HCU.
protocols.notes_updates: Time-critical: permanent brain damage after 30 min. Lorazepam → Phenytoin → Phenobarbital → Midazolam infusion + intubation.

management_steps.action: If conscious and cooperative: oral glucose
If unconscious or unable to swallow: Glucose 10% 100ml IV
If no IV access: Glucagon 1mg IM

drugs.notes: If 10% not available. High extravasation risk
If no IV access. Less effective in hepatic failure/alcoholism

protocols.disposition: Discharge if single episode, cause identified, and patient stable. Admit if sulfonylurea-induced or recurrent.
protocols.notes_updates: Glucose < 3.3 = hypoglycaemia. 10% glucose 100ml IV preferred. Glucagon 1mg IM if no IV access. Admit if sulfonylurea cause.

management_steps.action: Confirm hypertensive emergency (elevated BP + target organ damage)
management_steps.details: Distinguish from hypertensive urgency (no target organ damage)

management_steps.action: Reduce MAP by 20-25% in first hour

drugs.notes: Max 300mg. Avoid in asthma, heart block

warnings: Do not normalise BP too rapidly
Reduce MAP by 20-25% in first hour only
protocols.disposition: Admit HCU/Medicine for controlled BP reduction and monitoring.
protocols.notes_updates: Hypertensive emergency = elevated BP + target organ damage. Reduce MAP by 20-25% in first hour only. Avoid sublingual nifedipine.

lab: Paracetamol level (if suspected, at 4 hours post-ingestion)

management_steps.action: IV access, bloods including drug levels

management_steps.details: Activated charcoal 50g if alert and within 1-2 hours

drugs.notes: If within 1-2 hours and patient alert
For opioid overdose. May need infusion
For paracetamol overdose. Start within 8 hours

warnings: Do not rely on history alone - get drug levels
Activated charcoal not effective for all toxins
protocols.disposition: Admit to HCU/Medicine for observation and treatment.
protocols.notes_updates: Paracetamol: check level at 4 hours post-ingestion, start NAC if above treatment line. TCA: watch for QRS widening, sodium bicarbonate for QRS > 100ms. Opioid: naloxone 0.4-2mg IV.

imaging: Non-contrast CT brain (FIRST LINE - must be done immediately)

management_steps.action: Establish time of onset (last known well)
IV access, bloods (FBC, U&E, coagulation, troponin)
management_steps.caution: Do not delay CT for bloods

management_steps.action: If ischaemic and within 4.5 hours: Thrombolysis (Alteplase)
If haemorrhagic: reverse anticoagulation, neurosurgical consult
Aspirin 300mg stat (if not thrombolysed, after CT)

drugs.notes: 10% bolus, 90% over 60 min. Within 4.5 hours of onset
If not thrombolysed and CT excludes haemorrhage

warnings: Do not delay CT for bloods
Thrombolysis window: 4.5 hours for ischaemic stroke
protocols.disposition: Admit to stroke unit. Neurosurgical referral if haemorrhagic or large MCA infarct with oedema.
protocols.notes_updates: FAST assessment: Face, Arm, Speech, Time. CT brain must be done immediately. Thrombolysis within 4.5 hours if ischaemic.

signs: Wheeze (reduced or silent chest if severe)

management_steps.action: Prednisolone 40-50mg PO (or hydrocortisone 100mg IV if severe)
Admit if severe or not improving

protocols.disposition: Admit if severe, not improving after 1 hour, or life-threatening features. Discharge if PEF > 75% and stable 1 hour post-treatment.
protocols.notes_updates: PEF is key monitoring tool. Magnesium sulphate for severe cases. Early NIV if tiring.

management_steps.action: Prednisolone 30mg PO (or hydrocortisone 200mg IV if severe)
BiPAP if acidotic (pH < 7.35) or rising CO2
Admit if severe or not improving

protocols.disposition: Admit if severe, acidotic, or not improving. Consider early NIV.
protocols.notes_updates: Controlled O2 24-28% targeting 88-92%. BiPAP if acidotic. 5-day steroid course.

management_steps.action: IV access, bloods (U&E, creatinine, eGFR, FBC, VBG)

protocols.disposition: Admit to Medicine. Renal referral if severe or requiring dialysis.
protocols.notes_updates: Assess volume status first. Stop nephrotoxic drugs. Treat hyperkalaemia urgently. Renal USS for obstruction.

management_steps.details: Then 1L over 2 hours, then 1L over 4 hours, then 1L over 6 hours
Add KCl to fluids if K+ < 5.5 and patient passing urine

warnings: Fixed rate insulin only when K+ > 3.3
Never stop insulin if K+ low - replace K+ first
Bicarbonate rarely needed and potentially harmful
protocols.disposition: Admit to HCU/Medicine for insulin infusion and monitoring.
protocols.notes_updates: Fluid resuscitation is key: 1L/hr, then 1L/2hr, then 1L/4hr, then 1L/6hr. Insulin 0.1u/kg/hr when K+ > 3.3. Check VBG and U&E q2h.

management_steps.details: Slower than DKA - monitor for fluid overload

management_steps.action: Switch to 5% glucose when glucose < 14 mmol/L

warnings: Slower fluid and insulin correction than DKA
Monitor for cerebral oedema and thrombosis
protocols.disposition: Admit to HCU/Medicine. Higher mortality than DKA.
protocols.notes_updates: Slower correction than DKA. More cautious fluids. Target glucose fall 3-5 mmol/L/hr. Higher thrombosis risk.

warnings: Do not delay analgesia for surgical review
Beta-HCG in all females of childbearing age
protocols.disposition: Surgical referral. Admit for observation, imaging, and definitive management.
protocols.notes_updates: Nil by mouth, IV fluids, analgesia, imaging, surgical referral. Beta-HCG in all females.

imaging: X-rays (2 views, include joint above and below)

management_steps.details: Distal pulses, sensation, movement, capillary refill

management_steps.action: IV access, bloods, group and save
Open fractures: antibiotics, tetanus, photo, saline soak

warnings: Document neurovascular status before and after reduction
Open fractures need antibiotics within 1 hour
protocols.disposition: Orthopaedic referral. Admit for reduction and fixation.
protocols.notes_updates: Neurovascular assessment is critical. Open fractures need early antibiotics. Document pre- and post-reduction neurovascular status.

imaging: X-ray if foreign body or fracture suspected

management_steps.action: Explore for foreign bodies, tendon/nerve injury
X-ray if foreign body or fracture

protocols.disposition: Discharge with wound care instructions. Review in 48-72 hours if sutured.
protocols.notes_updates: Irrigation is key. Explore tendon/nerve function. X-ray for suspected foreign bodies. Antibiotics for bites and contaminated wounds.

management_steps.details: Parkland formula: 4ml x kg x %TBSA. Half in first 8 hours.

management_steps.action: Refer to burns unit if criteria met

protocols.disposition: Refer to burns unit if: TBSA > 10%, face/hands/genitalia/perineum, inhalation injury, electrical/chemical burns, extremes of age, comorbidities.
protocols.notes_updates: Parkland formula: 4ml x kg x %TBSA, half in first 8 hours. Cool running water for 20 min. Tetanus prophylaxis.

protocols.disposition: Refer to trauma team/surgery. Admit to ICU/HCU if severe.
protocols.notes_updates: ABCDE approach. C-spine control. eFAST. CT as indicated. TXA within 3 hours.

management_steps.action: CT brain if GCS < 15, focal neurology, or risk factors
Neurosurgical referral if CT positive or GCS ≤ 8

warnings: Do not miss extradural haematoma (lucid interval)
protocols.disposition: CT brain if indicated. Neurosurgical referral if positive CT or GCS ≤ 8. Admit if GCS < 15.
protocols.notes_updates: GCS is key. CT brain for moderate/severe. Anticoagulated patients need urgent CT. Watch for lucid interval in extradural haematoma.

components.name: PE is #1 diagnosis OR equally likely

protocols.disposition: Depends on cause. Admit for observation and treatment.
protocols.notes_updates: CXR first-line. CTPA for PE. CT aorta for dissection. Exclude cardiac causes.

imaging: CT brain if head injury or neurological deficits

management_steps.action: ABCs, vital signs including orthostatic BP
Discharge with follow-up if low risk

warnings: Admit if high risk by SFSR
protocols.disposition: Admit if high risk (SFSR positive, cardiac cause suspected). Discharge with follow-up if low risk vasovagal.
protocols.notes_updates: 12-lead ECG is mandatory. Orthostatic BP. SFSR for risk stratification. Cardiac syncope = highest risk.

management_steps.details: Then 1L over 2 hours, then 1L over 4 hours, then 1L over 6 hours
management_steps.caution: Monitor for fluid overload, especially elderly/CKD
Do not start if K+ < 3.3 - replace K+ first

management_steps.details: Add KCl to fluids: 20-40mmol/L if K+ < 5.5 and passing urine

management_steps.caution: Rarely needed - risks include cerebral oedema

warnings: Never stop insulin if K+ low - replace K+ first
Monitor for cerebral oedema (children, young adults)
protocols.disposition: Admit to HCU/Medicine for insulin infusion and monitoring.
protocols.notes_updates: Fluid resuscitation: 1L/hr, then 1L/2hr, then 1L/4hr, then 1L/6hr. Insulin 0.1u/kg/hr when K+ > 3.3. Check VBG and U&E q2h. Sliding scale when glucose < 14.

signs: ECG changes: peaked T waves, widened QRS, sine wave

management_steps.details: Cardioprotective. Repeat if ECG changes persist
management_steps.caution: Does not lower K+, just stabilises myocardium

management_steps.action: Actrapid insulin 10 units IV + 50ml 50% glucose IV over 15 min
Definitive: dialysis if refractory or renal failure

monitoring: Repeat K+ at 30 min, 1 hr, 2 hr
warnings: Calcium gluconate does not lower K+ - just protects heart
Insulin + glucose is temporary - need definitive removal
Dialysis if refractory or in renal failure
protocols.disposition: Admit to Medicine/ICU. Renal referral if dialysis needed.
protocols.notes_updates: C - Calcium (cardioprotective), G - Glucose + insulin, S - Salbutamol, B - Bicarbonate if acidotic. Repeat U&E at 30 min. Definitive removal with dialysis or resonium.

symptoms: Pain radiating to arm, jaw, back

management_steps.action: ABCs, general appearance, focused physical examination
12 & 15 lead ECG within 10 minutes of arrival

management_steps.details: If SBP > 90 and no RV infarct

management_steps.action: Morphine 2.5-5mg IV if pain not relieved by GTN
NSTE-ACS: risk stratify and manage accordingly

protocols.disposition: STEMI: thrombolysis + admit. NSTE-ACS: risk stratify, admit high risk.
protocols.notes_updates: ECG within 10 min. Aspirin 300mg stat. GTN if SBP > 90. STEMI thrombolysis if < 12 hours.

management_steps.details: Co-amoxiclav 1.2g IV TDS or cefuroxime + metronidazole

warnings: Do not delay surgical referral for imaging
Atypical presentations common in elderly and children
protocols.disposition: Surgical referral for appendicectomy. Admit.
protocols.notes_updates: Alvarado score for risk stratification. USS first-line imaging. Early surgical referral.

symptoms: Severe epigastric pain radiating to back

management_steps.action: CT abdomen with contrast at 48-72 hours if severe

warnings: Gallstone pancreatitis: early ERCP if obstructed/biliary sepsis
Aggressive fluid resuscitation in first 24 hours
Monitor for infected necrosis and organ failure
protocols.disposition: Admit to Medicine/ICU. Surgical referral if infected necrosis or complications.
protocols.notes_updates: Lipase > 3x ULN is diagnostic. Aggressive fluids in first 24 hours. CT at 48-72 hours. Glasgow score for severity.

interpretation.meaning: Low risk - may not need intervention

management_steps.action: Transfuse if Hb < 70-80 or ongoing bleed
Terlipressin 2mg IV if variceal bleed suspected

warnings: Rockall and Blatchford scores for risk stratification
protocols.disposition: Admit to HCU/Medicine. Urgent endoscopy. Surgical referral if ongoing.
protocols.notes_updates: 2 large bore cannulae. Crossmatch 4-6 units. PPI infusion for all. Terlipressin + octreotide for varices. Endoscopy within 24 hours.

management_steps.action: IV access, bloods if pyelonephritis or complicated
IV fluids if dehydrated or septic
Renal USS if complicated or not improving

warnings: Pregnant patients: avoid nitrofurantoin in 3rd trimester
Renal USS if complicated or obstruction suspected
protocols.disposition: Uncomplicated UTI: discharge with antibiotics. Pyelonephritis: admit for IV antibiotics.
protocols.notes_updates: Urine MC&S before antibiotics. Nitrofurantoin for cystitis. Co-amoxiclav/ceftriaxone for pyelonephritis. Renal USS if complicated.

lab: LP (CSF analysis - glucose, protein, cell count, culture, PCR)
Coagulation before LP if suspected meningococcal
imaging: CT brain before LP if: papilloedema, focal neurology, immunocompromised, new-onset seizures, altered GCS

management_steps.action: IV access, bloods including blood cultures
Empiric antibiotics within 1 hour (do not delay for LP/CT)
Dexamethasone 10mg IV before or with first antibiotic dose
CT brain before LP if indicated
management_steps.caution: Do not delay antibiotics for CT

warnings: Antibiotics within 1 hour - do not delay for LP/CT
CT before LP if indicated but don't delay antibiotics
protocols.disposition: Admit to ICU/HCU. Notify public health if meningococcal.
protocols.notes_updates: Antibiotics within 1 hour. Dexamethasone 10mg IV with first antibiotic. CT before LP if indicated. Aciclovir if HSV suspected.

lab: Blood cultures x2 (peripheral and central line)

management_steps.action: IV access, bloods including blood cultures
Add vancomycin if line infection or MRSA risk

drugs.notes: If line infection or MRSA risk

warnings: Empiric broad-spectrum - no waiting for cultures
Consider fungal coverage if persistent fever after 4-5 days
protocols.disposition: Admit to isolation/ICU. Haematology/oncology referral.
protocols.notes_updates: Empiric piperacillin-tazobactam within 1 hour. Add vancomycin for line infection. Add metronidazole for GI source. G-CSF if appropriate.

management_steps.action: ABCs, 2 large bore cannulae if shocked
IV access, bloods including group and save
Surgery if ruptured or haemodynamically unstable

warnings: All females of childbearing age with abdominal pain: pregnancy test
Methotrexate only if strict criteria met
protocols.disposition: Gynaecology referral. Surgery if ruptured/unstable. Methotrexate if stable with strict criteria.
protocols.notes_updates: Pregnancy test in all females of childbearing age with abdominal pain. USS first-line. Gynaecology referral urgently. Methotrexate criteria: stable, unruptured, beta-HCG < 5000, mass < 3cm, no fetal heartbeat.

imaging: CT brain if first presentation or organic cause suspected

management_steps.action: Ensure safety of patient and staff

warnings: Lorazepam preferred over haloperidol (less extrapyramidal side effects)
protocols.disposition: Psychiatric admission if high risk. Discharge with follow-up if low risk with support.
protocols.notes_updates: Safety first. Medical clearance. Risk assessment. Lorazepam for agitation. Psychiatric referral.

management_steps.action: Dexamethasone 6mg OD if O2 requirement or CRP > 75
Remdesivir if severe (O2 requirement) and within 7 days of symptom onset
Admit if O2 requirement or significant comorbidities

drugs.notes: If severe and within 7 days of symptoms

protocols.disposition: Admit if O2 requirement or high risk. Home if mild and low risk.
protocols.notes_updates: Dexamethasone 6mg OD if O2 requirement. Remdesivir if severe and early. LMWH for VTE prophylaxis. NEWS2 for monitoring.

imaging: CT brain if head trauma or focal neurology

management_steps.action: Chemical sedation if danger to self/others
Avoid antipsychotics if Lewy body dementia or Parkinson's

warnings: Avoid antipsychotics in Lewy body dementia
Safety of patient and staff paramount
protocols.disposition: Admit for underlying cause workup and management.
protocols.notes_updates: CAM for assessment. Non-pharmacological first. Lorazepam for sedation. Always search for underlying cause.

components.name: Recently bedridden > 3 days or major surgery < 4 weeks
Calf swelling > 3cm compared to asymptomatic leg
Alternative diagnosis as likely or more likely

management_steps.action: If D-dimer positive: compression duplex USS
Clexane 1mg/kg SC BD (or 1.5mg/kg OD) OR DOAC
management_steps.details: Apixaban 10mg BD x 7 days then 5mg BD

management_steps.action: Admit if massive DVT, phlegmasia, or comorbidities

warnings: Always check for contraindications to anticoagulation
Follow algorithm - do not skip steps
protocols.disposition: Most DVTs can be treated as outpatients. Admit if massive, phlegmasia, or significant comorbidities.
protocols.notes_updates: Wells score first. USS if Wells ≥ 2. D-dimer if Wells < 2. Start Clexane or DOAC if confirmed. Follow algorithm - do not skip steps; If DVT positive, always start Clexane stat; Repeat formal ultrasound in 7 days if initial testing inconclusive; Consider malignancy workup in unprovoked DVT
